text.Rotherham: and took away the foreign altars, and the high places,—and brake in pieces the pillars, and cut in twain the Sacred Stems;
text.KJV: For he took away the altars of the strange gods, and the high places, and brake down the images, and cut down the groves:
text.NABRE: He told Judah to seek the Lord, the God of their ancestors, and to observe the law and the commandment.
text.DRB: and brake the statues, and cut down the groves.

text.NRSV: and commanded Judah to seek the Lord, the God of their ancestors, and to keep the law and the commandment.
text.Rotherham: and bade Judah seek Yahweh, God of their fathers,-and execute the law, and the commandment;
text.KJV: And commanded Judah to seek the Lord God of their fathers, and to do the law and the commandment.
text.NABRE: He removed the high places and incense stands from all the cities of Judah, and under him the kingdom had peace.
text.DRB: And he commanded Juda that they should seek our Lord the God of their fathers, and should do the law, and all the commandments.

text.NRSV: He also removed from all the cities of Judah the high places and the incense altars. And the kingdom had rest under him.
text.Rotherham: and removed from all the cities of Judah, the high places, and the sun-images,—and the kingdom became quiet before him.
text.KJV: Also he took away out of all the cities of Judah the high places and the images: and the kingdom was quiet before him.
text.NABRE: He built fortified cities in Judah, for the land had peace and no war was waged against him during these years, because the Lord had given him rest.
text.DRB: And he took away out of all the cities of Juda the altars, & temples, and reigned in peace.

text.NRSV: He built fortified cities in Judah while the land had rest. He had no war in those years, for the Lord gave him peace.
text.Rotherham: And he built cities of defence, in Judah,—for the land had quiet, nor was there near him any war, in these years, because Yahweh had given him rest.
text.KJV: And he built fenced cities in Judah: for the land had rest, and he had no war in those years; because the Lord had given him rest.
text.NABRE: He said to Judah: “Let us build these cities and surround them with walls, towers, gates and bars. The land is still ours, for we have sought the Lord, our God; we sought him, and he has given us rest on every side.” So they built and prospered. The Ethiopian Invasion.
text.DRB: He built also fenced cities in Juda, because he was quiet, and there had no battles risen in his time, our Lord giving peace.

text.NRSV: He said to Judah, “Let us build these cities, and surround them with walls and towers, gates and bars; the land is still ours because we have sought the Lord our God; we have sought him, and he has given us peace on every side.” So they built and prospered.
text.Rotherham: Therefore said he to Judah, Let us build these cities, and let us surround them with walls and towers, doors and bars, while the land is yet before us, for we have sought Yahweh our God, we have sought him, and he hath given us rest on every side. So they built, and prospered.
text.KJV: Therefore he said unto Judah, Let us build these cities, and make about them walls, and towers, gates, and bars, while the land is yet before us; because we have sought the Lord our God, we have sought him, and he hath given us rest on every side. So they built and prospered.
text.NABRE: Asa had an army of three hundred thousand shield- and lance-bearers from Judah, and from Benjamin two hundred and eighty thousand who carried bucklers and were archers, all of them valiant warriors.
text.DRB: And he said to Juda: let us build these cities, and compass them with walls, and strengthen them with towers, and gates, & locks, whilst things are quiet from battles, because we have sought our Lord the God of our fathers, and he hath given us peace round about. They therefore did build, and there was no impediment in building.

text.NRSV: Asa had an army of three hundred thousand from Judah, armed with large shields and spears, and two hundred eighty thousand troops from Benjamin who carried shields and drew bows; all these were mighty warriors.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, Asa, had a force bearing shield and spear, out of Judah, three hundred thousand, and, out of Benjamin, such as bare a buckler and trode a bow, two hundred and eighty thousand,—all these, were heroes of valour.
text.KJV: And Asa had an army of men that bare targets and spears, out of Judah three hundred thousand; and out of Benjamin, that bare shields and drew bows, two hundred and fourscore thousand: all these were mighty men of valour.
text.NABRE: Zerah the Ethiopian advanced against them with a force of one million men and three hundred chariots, and he came as far as Mareshah.
text.DRB: And Asa had in his army of them that carried shields and spears, of Juda three hundred thousand: and of Benjamin shield-bearers and archers, two hundred eighty thousand, all these were most valiant men.

text.NRSV: Zerah the Ethiopian came out against them with an army of a million men and three hundred chariots, and came as far as Mareshah.
text.Rotherham: Then came out against them Zerah the Ethiopian, with a force of a thousand thousand, and three hundred chariots,—and he came as far as Mareshah.
text.KJV: And there came out against them Zerah the Ethiopian with an host of a thousand thousand, and three hundred chariots; and came unto Mareshah.
text.NABRE: Asa went out to meet him and they drew up for battle in the valley of Zephathah, near Mareshah.
text.DRB: And Zara the Æthiopian with his army issued forth against them, ten hundred thousand, and with three hundred chariots: and he came as far as Maresa.

text.NRSV: Asa went out to meet him, and they drew up their lines of battle in the valley of Zephathah at Mareshah.
text.Rotherham: And Asa went out to meet him,—and they set in array for battle, in the valley of Zaphonah, at Mareshah.
text.KJV: Then Asa went out against him, and they set the battle in array in the valley of Zephathah at Mareshah.
text.NABRE: Asa called upon the Lord, his God: “Lord, there is none like you to help the powerless against the strong. Help us, Lord, our God, for we rely on you, and in your name we have come against this multitude. You are the Lord, our God; do not let men prevail against you.”
text.DRB: Moreover Asa went on to meet him, and set his army in array to battle in the vale Sephata, which is near Maresa.

text.NRSV: Asa cried to the Lord his God, “O Lord, there is no difference for you between helping the mighty and the weak. Help us, O Lord our God, for we rely on you, and in your name we have come against this multitude. O Lord, you are our God; let no mortal prevail against you.”
text.Rotherham: Then Asa cried out unto Yahweh his God, and said, O Yahweh, it is, nothing with thee, to help whether with many or with such as have no strength. Help us, O Yahweh our God, for, on thee, do we lean, and, in thy name, have we come against this multitude,—O Yahweh! our God, thou art, let not, weak man, have power against thee.
text.KJV: And Asa cried unto the Lord his God, and said, Lord, it is nothing with thee to help, whether with many, or with them that have no power: help us, O Lord our God; for we rest on thee, and in thy name we go against this multitude. O Lord, thou art our God; let not man prevail against thee.
text.NABRE: And so the Lord defeated the Ethiopians before Asa and Judah, and the Ethiopians fled.
text.DRB: And he invocated our Lord God, and said: Lord there is no difference with thee, whether thou help in few, or in many: help us o Lord our God: for having confidence in thee, & in thy name we are come against this multitude. Lord, thou art our God, let not man prevail against thee.

text.NRSV: So the Lord defeated the Ethiopians before Asa and before Judah, and the Ethiopians fled.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh smote the Ethiopians, before Asa, and before Judah,—and the Ethiopians fled.
text.KJV: So the Lord smote the Ethiopians before Asa, and before Judah; and the Ethiopians fled.
text.NABRE: Asa and those with him pursued them as far as Gerar, and the Ethiopians fell until there were no survivors, for they were crushed before the Lord and his army, which carried away enormous spoils.
text.DRB: Our Lord therefore terrified the Æthiopians before Asa and Juda: & the Æthiopians fled.

text.NRSV: Asa and the army with him pursued them as far as Gerar, and the Ethiopians fell until no one remained alive; for they were broken before the Lord and his army. The people of Judah carried away a great quantity of booty.
text.Rotherham: And Asa and the people that were with him pursued as far as to Gerar, and the Ethiopians fell, so that there was no way for them to recover, for they were routed before Yahweh, and before him host,—and they carried away exceeding much spoil.
text.KJV: And Asa and the people that were with him pursued them unto Gerar: and the Ethiopians were overthrown, that they could not recover themselves; for they were destroyed before the Lord, and before his host; and they carried away very much spoil.
text.NABRE: Then the Judahites conquered all the cities around Gerar, for the fear of the Lord was upon them; they plundered all the cities, for there was much plunder in them.
text.DRB: And Asa pursued them, & the people that was with him, unto Gerara: & the Æthiopians fell to utter destruction because our Lord killing them, and his army fighting, they were destroyed. They took therefore many spoils,

text.NRSV: They defeated all the cities around Gerar, for the fear of the Lord was on them. They plundered all the cities; for there was much plunder in them.
text.Rotherham: And they smote all the cities round shout Gerar, for the dread of Yahweh was upon them,—and they plundered all the cities, for, great plunder, was there in them.
text.KJV: And they smote all the cities round about Gerar; for the fear of the Lord came upon them: and they spoiled all the cities; for there was exceeding much spoil in them.
text.NABRE: They also attacked the tents of the cattle-herders and carried off a great number of sheep and camels. Then they returned to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: and they struck all the cities round about Gerara: for great terror had invaded all men: and they spoiled the cities, and carried away much prey.

text.NRSV: They also attacked the tents of those who had livestock, and carried away sheep and goats in abundance, and camels. Then they returned to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, the tents of the cattle, they smote,—and carried off sheep in abundance, and camels, and returned to Jerusalem.
text.KJV: They smote also the tents of cattle, and carried away sheep and camels in abundance, and returned to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Yea and destroying the sheepcotes of sheep, they took an infinite multitude of cattle, and of camels: and returned into Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: The spirit of God came upon Azariah son of Oded.
text.Rotherham: Now, as for Azariah son of Oded, there came upon him, the spirit of God.
text.KJV: And the Spirit of God came upon Azariah the son of Oded:
text.NABRE: The spirit of God came upon Azariah, son of Oded.
text.DRB: And Azarias the son of Oded, the Spirit of God coming upon him,

text.NRSV: He went out to meet Asa and said to him, “Hear me, Asa, and all Judah and Benjamin: The Lord is with you, while you are with him. If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you abandon him, he will abandon you.
text.Rotherham: So he went forth to meet Asa, and said unto him, Hear me, O Asa, and all Judah and Benjamin,—Yahweh, is with you, so long as ye are with him, and, if ye seek him, he will be found of you, but, if ye forsake him, he will forsake you.
text.KJV: And he went out to meet Asa, and said unto him, Hear ye me, Asa, and all Judah and Benjamin; The Lord is with you, while ye be with him; and if ye seek him, he will be found of you; but if ye forsake him, he will forsake you.
text.NABRE: He went forth to meet Asa and said to him: “Hear me, Asa and all Judah and Benjamin! The Lord is with you when you are with him, and if you seek him he will be found; but if you abandon him, he will abandon you.
text.DRB: went out to meet Asa, and said to him: Hear ye me Asa, and all Juda and Benjamin: Our Lord is with you, because you have been with him. If you will seek him, you shall find: but if you forsake him, he will forsake you.

text.NRSV: For a long time Israel was without the true God, and without a teaching priest, and without law;
text.Rotherham: Now, many days, had Israel been,—without the faithful God, and without a teaching priest, and without the law;
text.KJV: Now for a long season Israel hath been without the true God, and without a teaching priest, and without law.
text.NABRE: For a long time Israel was without a true God, without a priest-teacher, without instruction,
text.DRB: And many days shall pass in Israel without the true God, and without Priest a teacher, and without the Law.

text.NRSV: but when in their distress they turned to the Lord, the God of Israel, and sought him, he was found by them.
text.Rotherham: But they turned, in their distress, unto Yahweh God of Israel,—and sought him, and he was found of them.
text.KJV: But when they in their trouble did turn unto the Lord God of Israel, and sought him, he was found of them.
text.NABRE: but when in their distress they turned to the Lord, the God of Israel, and sought him, he was found by them.
text.DRB: And when they shall return in their distress to our Lord the God of Israel, and shall seek him, they shall find him.

text.NRSV: In those times it was not safe for anyone to go or come, for great disturbances afflicted all the inhabitants of the lands.
text.Rotherham: And, in those times, there had been no prosperity, to him that went out nor to him that came in,—for, great consternations, were upon all the inhabitants of the lands;
text.KJV: And in those times there was no peace to him that went out, nor to him that came in, but great vexations were upon all the inhabitants of the countries.
text.NABRE: At that time there was no peace for anyone to go or come; rather, there were many terrors upon the inhabitants of the lands.
text.DRB: At that time there shall not be peace to him that goeth out and cometh in, but terrors on every side in the inhabiters of the earth.

text.NRSV: They were broken in pieces, nation against nation and city against city, for God troubled them with every sort of distress.
text.Rotherham: and they were beaten in pieces-nation against nation, and city against city,—for, God, discomfited them with all manner of distress.
text.KJV: And nation was destroyed of nation, and city of city: for God did vex them with all adversity.
text.NABRE: Nation crushed nation and city crushed city, for God overwhelmed them with every kind of distress.
text.DRB: For nation shall fight against nation, and city against city, because our Lord will trouble them with all distress.

text.NRSV: But you, take courage! Do not let your hands be weak, for your work shall be rewarded.”
text.Rotherham: Ye, therefore, be strong, and let not your hands be slack,—for there is a reward for your work!
text.KJV: Be ye strong therefore, and let not your hands be weak: for your work shall be rewarded.
text.NABRE: But as for you, be strong and do not slack off, for there shall be a reward for what you do.”
text.DRB: You therefore take courage, and let not your hands be dissolved: for there shall be reward to your work.

text.NRSV: When Asa heard these words, the prophecy of Azariah son of Oded, he took courage, and put away the abominable idols from all the land of Judah and Benjamin and from the towns that he had taken in the hill country of Ephraim. He repaired the altar of the Lord that was in front of the vestibule of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, when Asa heard these words and the prophecy of Oded the prophet, he strengthened himself, and put away the abominations out of all the land of Judah and Benjamin, and out of the cities which he had captured out of the hill country of Ephraim,—and renewed the altar of Yahweh, that was before the porch of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And when Asa heard these words, and the prophecy of Oded the prophet, he took courage, and put away the abominable idols out of all the land of Judah and Benjamin, and out of the cities which he had taken from mount Ephraim, and renewed the altar of the Lord, that was before the porch of the Lord.
text.NABRE: When Asa heard these words and the prophecy (Oded the prophet), he was encouraged to remove the detestable idols from the whole land of Judah and Benjamin and from the cities he had taken in the highlands of Ephraim, and to restore the altar of the Lord which was before the vestibule of the Lord.
text.DRB: Which when Asa had heard, to wit, the words, and the prophecy of Azarias the son of Oded the Prophet, he took courage, and took away the idols out of all the land of Juda, and out of Benjamin, and out of the cities, which he had taken, of mount Ephraim, and he dedicated the altar of our Lord, which was before the porch of our Lord.

text.NRSV: He gathered all Judah and Benjamin, and those from Ephraim, Manasseh, and Simeon who were residing as aliens with them, for great numbers had deserted to him from Israel when they saw that the Lord his God was with him.
text.Rotherham: And he gathered together all Judah and Benjamin, and the sojourners with them, out of Ephraim and out of Manasseh, and out of Simeon,—for they fell unto him out of Israel, in great numbers, because they saw that, Yahweh his God, was with him.
text.KJV: And he gathered all Judah and Benjamin, and the strangers with them out of Ephraim and Manasseh, and out of Simeon: for they fell to him out of Israel in abundance, when they saw that the Lord his God was with him.
text.NABRE: Then he gathered all Judah and Benjamin, together with those of Ephraim, Manasseh, and Simeon who were resident with them; for many had defected to him from Israel when they saw that the Lord, his God, was with him.
text.DRB: And he gathered together all Juda and Benjamin, & the strangers with them of Ephraim, and of Manasses, and of Simeon: for many were fled to him of Israel, seeing that our Lord his God was with him.

text.NRSV: They were gathered at Jerusalem in the third month of the fifteenth year of the reign of Asa.
text.Rotherham: So they gathered themselves together unto Jerusalem, in the third month of the fifteenth year of the reign of Asa.
text.KJV: So they gathered themselves together at Jerusalem in the third month, in the fifteenth year of the reign of Asa.
text.NABRE: They gathered at Jerusalem in the third month of the fifteenth year of Asa’s reign,
text.DRB: And when they were come into Jerusalem the third month, in the fifteenth year of the reign of Asa,

text.NRSV: They sacrificed to the Lord on that day, from the booty that they had brought, seven hundred oxen and seven thousand sheep.
text.Rotherham: And they sacrificed unto Yahweh, on that day, out of the spoil they had brought in,—oxen, seven hundred, and sheep, seven thousand.
text.KJV: And they offered unto the Lord the same time, of the spoil which they had brought, seven hundred oxen and seven thousand sheep.
text.NABRE: and sacrificed to the Lord on that day seven hundred oxen and seven thousand sheep from the spoils they had brought.
text.DRB: they immolated to our Lord in that day of the spoils and the prey that they had brought, oxen seven hundred, and rams seven thousand.

text.NRSV: They entered into a covenant to seek the Lord, the God of their ancestors, with all their heart and with all their soul.
text.Rotherham: And they entered into a covenant, to seek Yahweh, God of their fathers,—with all their heart, and with all their soul;
text.KJV: And they entered into a covenant to seek the Lord God of their fathers with all their heart and with all their soul;
text.NABRE: They entered into a covenant to seek the Lord, the God of their ancestors, with all their heart and soul;
text.DRB: And he went in after the manner to establish the covenant, that they should seek our Lord the God of their fathers in all their heart, and in all their soul.

text.NRSV: Whoever would not seek the Lord, the God of Israel, should be put to death, whether young or old, man or woman.
text.Rotherham: and, whosoever would not seek unto Yahweh God of Israel, should be put to death,—whether small or great, whether man or woman.
text.KJV: That whosoever would not seek the Lord God of Israel should be put to death, whether small or great, whether man or woman.
text.NABRE: and everyone who would not seek the Lord, the God of Israel, was to be put to death, from least to greatest, man or woman.
text.DRB: And if any man, quoth he, shall not seek our Lord the God of Israel, let him die, from the least to the greatest, from man unto woman.

text.NRSV: They took an oath to the Lord with a loud voice, and with shouting, and with trumpets, and with horns.
text.Rotherham: So they bound themselves by oath unto Yahweh, with a loud voice, and with shouting,—and with trumpets and with horns.
text.KJV: And they sware unto the Lord with a loud voice, and with shouting, and with trumpets, and with cornets.
text.NABRE: They swore an oath to the Lord with a loud voice, with shouting and with trumpets and horns.
text.DRB: And they sware to our Lord with a loud voice in jubilation, and in noise of trumpet, and sound of shalms,

text.NRSV: All Judah rejoiced over the oath; for they had sworn with all their heart, and had sought him with their whole desire, and he was found by them, and the Lord gave them rest all around.
text.Rotherham: And all Judah rejoiced over the oath, for, with all their heart, had they sworn, and, with all their good will, had they sought him, and he was found of them,—and Yahweh gave them rest round about.
text.KJV: And all Judah rejoiced at the oath: for they had sworn with all their heart, and sought him with their whole desire; and he was found of them: and the Lord gave them rest round about.
text.NABRE: All Judah rejoiced over the oath, for they had sworn it with their whole heart and sought him with complete desire. The Lord was found by them, and gave them rest on every side.
text.DRB: all that were in Juda, with execration: for in all their heart did they swear, & with all their will did they seek him, & found him, & our Lord gave them rest round about.

text.NRSV: King Asa even removed his mother Maacah from being queen mother because she had made an abominable image for Asherah. Asa cut down her image, crushed it, and burned it at the Wadi Kidron.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, as touching Maacah mother of Asa the king, he removed her from being queen, because she had made, unto the Sacred Stem, a monstrous thing,—so Asa cut down her monstrous thing, and reduced it to dust, and burnt it up, in the Kidron ravine.
text.KJV: And also concerning Maachah the mother of Asa the king, he removed her from being queen, because she had made an idol in a grove: and Asa cut down her idol, and stamped it, and burnt it at the brook Kidron.
text.NABRE: He also deposed Maacah, the mother of King Asa, from her position as queen mother because she had made an obscene object for Asherah; Asa cut down this object, smashed it, and burnt it in the Wadi Kidron.
text.DRB: Yea & Maacha the mother of king Asa he deposed from the royal empire, because she had made in a grove the idol of Priapus: which he wholly destroyed, and breaking into pieces, burnt it in the Torrent cedron.

text.NRSV: But the high places were not taken out of Israel. Nevertheless the heart of Asa was true all his days.
text.Rotherham: But, the high places, were not taken away out of Israel,—only, the heart of Asa, was perfect, all his days.
text.KJV: But the high places were not taken away out of Israel: nevertheless the heart of Asa was perfect all his days.
text.NABRE: The high places did not disappear from Israel, yet Asa’s heart was undivided as long as he lived.
text.DRB: But the Excelses were left in Israel: nevertheless the heart of Asa was perfect all his days.

text.NRSV: He brought into the house of God the votive gifts of his father and his own votive gifts—silver, gold, and utensils.
text.Rotherham: And he brought the hallowed things of his father and his own hallowed things, into the house of God,—silver and gold, and utensils.
text.KJV: And he brought into the house of God the things that his father had dedicated, and that he himself had dedicated, silver, and gold, and vessels.
text.NABRE: He brought into the house of God his father’s and his own votive offerings: silver, gold, and vessels.
text.DRB: And those things which his father had vowed, and himself, he brought into the house of our Lord, gold and silver, and of vessels diverse furniture.

text.NRSV: And there was no more war until the thirty-fifth year of the reign of Asa.
text.Rotherham: And, war, was there none,—until the thirty-fifth year of the reign of Asa.
text.KJV: And there was no more war unto the five and thirtieth year of the reign of Asa.
text.NABRE: There was no war until the thirty-fifth year of Asa’s reign.
text.DRB: And there was no war unto the five and thirtieth year of the reign of Asa.

text.NRSV: In the thirty-sixth year of the reign of Asa, King Baasha of Israel went up against Judah, and built Ramah, to prevent anyone from going out or coming into the territory of King Asa of Judah.
text.Rotherham: In the thirty-sixth year of the reign of Asa, Baasha king of Israel came up against Judah, and built Ramah,—that he might let none come out or go in, unto Asa king of Judah.
text.KJV: In the six and thirtieth year of the reign of Asa Baasha king of Israel came up against Judah, and built Ramah, to the intent that he might let none go out or come in to Asa king of Judah.
text.NABRE: In the thirty-sixth year of Asa’s reign, Baasha, king of Israel, attacked Judah and fortified Ramah to block all movement for Asa, king of Judah.
text.DRB: And in the six and thirtieth year of his reign, came up Baasa the king of Israel into Juda, and with a wall compassed Rama, that none could safely go out and come in of the Kingdom of Asa.

text.NRSV: Then Asa took silver and gold from the treasures of the house of the Lord and the king’s house, and sent them to King Ben-hadad of Aram, who resided in Damascus, saying,
text.Rotherham: So Asa brought forth silver and gold, out of the treasuries of the house of Yahweh, and the house of the king,—and sent unto Ben-hadad, king of Syria, who dwelt in Damascus, saying:
text.KJV: Then Asa brought out silver and gold out of the treasures of the house of the Lord and of the king’s house, and sent to Ben–hadad king of Syria, that dwelt at Damascus, saying,
text.NABRE: Asa then brought out silver and gold from the treasuries of the house of the Lord and the house of the king and sent them to Ben-hadad, king of Aram, who ruled in Damascus. He said:
text.DRB: Asa therefore brought forth silver and gold, out of the treasures house of our Lord, and of the king's treasures, and he sent to Benadad the king of Syria, who dwelt in Damascus, saying:

text.NRSV: “Let there be an alliance between me and you, like that between my father and your father; I am sending to you silver and gold; go, break your alliance with King Baasha of Israel, so that he may withdraw from me.”
text.Rotherham: [Let there be] a covenant, between me and thee, as between my father and thy father.—Lo! I have sent thee silver and gold, go break thy covenant with Baasha king of Israel, that he may go up from me.
text.KJV: There is a league between me and thee, as there was between my father and thy father: behold, I have sent thee silver and gold; go, break thy league with Baasha king of Israel, that he may depart from me.
text.NABRE: “There is a treaty between you and me, as there was between your father and my father. I am sending you silver and gold. Go, break your treaty with Baasha, king of Israel, that he may withdraw from me.”
text.DRB: There is league between me and thee, my father also & thy father had concord, wherefore I have sent thee silver and gold that breaking the league, which thou hast with Baasa the king of Israel, thou make him retire from me.

text.NRSV: Ben-hadad listened to King Asa, and sent the commanders of his armies against the cities of Israel. They conquered Ijon, Dan, Abel-maim, and all the store-cities of Naphtali.
text.Rotherham: And Ben-hadad hearkened unto King Asa, and sent the chieftains of the forces which he had, against the cities of Israel, and smote Ijon and Dan, and Abel-maim,—and all the storehouses of the cities of Naphtali.
text.KJV: And Ben–hadad hearkened unto king Asa, and sent the captains of his armies against the cities of Israel; and they smote Ijon, and Dan, and Abel–maim, and all the store cities of Naphtali.
text.NABRE: Ben-hadad agreed with King Asa and sent the leaders of his troops against the cities of Israel. They attacked Ijon, Dan, Abel-maim, besides all the store cities of Naphtali.
text.DRB: Which being known, Benadad sent the Princes of his hosts to the cities of Israel: who struck Ahion, and Dan, and Ablemaim, and all the walled cities of Nephthali.

text.NRSV: When Baasha heard of it, he stopped building Ramah, and let his work cease.
text.Rotherham: And it cams to pass, when Baasha heard it, he left off building Ramah,—and let his work cease.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when Baasha heard it, that he left off building of Ramah, and let his work cease.
text.NABRE: When Baasha heard of it, he left off fortifying Ramah, putting an end to his work.
text.DRB: Which when Baasa had heard, he ceased to build Rama, & intermitted his work.

text.NRSV: Then King Asa brought all Judah, and they carried away the stones of Ramah and its timber, with which Baasha had been building, and with them he built up Geba and Mizpah.
text.Rotherham: And, Asa the king, took all Judah, and they carried away the stones of Ramah, and the timbers thereof, which Baasha had used in building,—and he built therewith, Geba and Mizpah.
text.KJV: Then Asa the king took all Judah; and they carried away the stones of Ramah, and the timber thereof, wherewith Baasha was building; and he built therewith Geba and Mizpah.
text.NABRE: Then King Asa commandeered all Judah and they carried away the stones and beams with which Baasha was fortifying Ramah. With them he fortified Geba and Mizpah.
text.DRB: Moreover Asa the king took all Juda, and carried away the stones out of Rama, and the timber that Baasa had prepared for the building: & he built of them Gabaa, & Maspha.

text.NRSV: At that time the seer Hanani came to King Asa of Judah, and said to him, “Because you relied on the king of Aram, and did not rely on the Lord your God, the army of the king of Aram has escaped you.
text.Rotherham: And, at that time, came Hanani the seer, unto Asa the king of Judah,—and said unto him, Because thou hast leaned upon the king of Syria, and hast not leaned upon Yahweh thy God, for this cause, hath the force of the king of Syria escaped out of thy hand.
text.KJV: And at that time Hanani the seer came to Asa king of Judah, and said unto him, Because thou hast relied on the king of Syria, and not relied on the Lord thy God, therefore is the host of the king of Syria escaped out of thine hand.
text.NABRE: At that time Hanani the seer came to Asa, king of Judah, and said to him: “Because you relied on the king of Aram and did not rely on the Lord, your God, the army of the king of Aram has escaped your power.
text.DRB: At that time came Hanani the Prophet to Asa the king of Juda, & said to him: Because thou hast had confidence in the king of Syria, and not in our Lord thy God, therefore hath the army of the king of Syria escaped out of thy hand.

text.NRSV: Were not the Ethiopians and the Libyans a huge army with exceedingly many chariots and cavalry? Yet because you relied on the Lord, he gave them into your hand.
text.Rotherham: Were not, the Ethiopians and the Lybians, a huge host, with chariots and horsemen exceeding many? yet, because thou didst lean upon Yahweh, he delivered them into thy hand.
text.KJV: Were not the Ethiopians and the Lubims a huge host, with very many chariots and horsemen? yet, because thou didst rely on the Lord, he delivered them into thine hand.
text.NABRE: Were not the Ethiopians and Libyans a vast army, with great numbers of chariots and horses? And yet, because you relied on the Lord, he delivered them into your power.
text.DRB: Were not the Æthiopians, and Libyans, many more in chariots and horsemen, and a multitude exceeding great: whom, when thou didst believe in our Lord, he delivered into thy hand?

text.NRSV: For the eyes of the Lord range throughout the entire earth, to strengthen those whose heart is true to him. You have done foolishly in this; for from now on you will have wars.”
text.Rotherham: For, as touching Yahweh, [seeing that], his eyes, are ever running to and fro throughout all the earth, to shew himself strong with them who are perfect toward himself, thou hast made thyself foolish over this,—for, from henceforth, there shall be with thee—wars.
text.KJV: For the eyes of the Lord run to and fro throughout the whole earth, to shew himself strong in the behalf of them whose heart is perfect toward him. Herein thou hast done foolishly: therefore from henceforth thou shalt have wars.
text.NABRE: The eyes of the Lord roam over the whole earth, to encourage those who are devoted to him wholeheartedly. You have acted foolishly in this matter, for from now on you will have wars.”
text.DRB: For the eyes of our Lord behold all the earth, and give strength to them, that with perfect heart believe in him. Thou therefore hast done foolishly, and for this cause from this present time shall battles arise against thee.

text.NRSV: Then Asa was angry with the seer, and put him in the stocks, in prison, for he was in a rage with him because of this. And Asa inflicted cruelties on some of the people at the same time.
text.Rotherham: Then was Asa angry with the seer, and put him in the house of the stocks, for he was in a rage with him, over this,—and Asa oppressed some of the people, at that time.
text.KJV: Then Asa was wroth with the seer, and put him in a prison house; for he was in a rage with him because of this thing. And Asa oppressed some of the people the same time.
text.NABRE: But Asa became angry with the seer and imprisoned him in the stocks, so greatly was he enraged at him over this. Asa also oppressed some of his people at this time.
text.DRB: And Asa being angry against the Seer, commanded him to be cast into fetters: for he took indignation exceedingly upon this thing: & he slew of the people at that time very many.

text.NRSV: The acts of Asa, from first to last, are written in the Book of the Kings of Judah and Israel.
text.Rotherham: But lo! the story of Asa, first and last, there it is, written in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.
text.KJV: And, behold, the acts of Asa, first and last, lo, they are written in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.
text.NABRE: Now the acts of Asa, first and last, are recorded in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.
text.DRB: But the works of Asa the first & the last are written in the Book of the kings of Juda and Israel.

text.NRSV: In the thirty-ninth year of his reign Asa was diseased in his feet, and his disease became severe; yet even in his disease he did not seek the Lord, but sought help from physicians.
text.Rotherham: And Asa became diseased—in the thirty-ninth year of his reign—in his feet, exceedingly severe, was his disease,—yet, even in his disease, he sought not Yahweh, but unto physicians.
text.KJV: And Asa in the thirty and ninth year of his reign was diseased in his feet, until his disease was exceeding great: yet in his disease he sought not to the Lord, but to the physicians.
text.NABRE: In the thirty-ninth year of his reign, Asa contracted disease in his feet; it became worse, but even with this disease he did not seek the Lord, only physicians.
text.DRB: Asa also fell sick in the nine and thirtieth year of his reign, of a most vehement pain of his feet, and neither in his infirmity did he seek our Lord, but rather trusted in the art of Physicians.

text.NRSV: Then Asa slept with his ancestors, dying in the forty-first year of his reign.
text.Rotherham: And Asa slept with his fathers,—yea he died in the forty-first year of his reign;
text.KJV: And Asa slept with his fathers, and died in the one and fortieth year of his reign.
text.NABRE: Asa rested with his ancestors; he died in the forty-first year of his reign.
text.DRB: And he slept with his fathers: and he died the one and fortieth year of his reign.

text.NRSV: They buried him in the tomb that he had hewn out for himself in the city of David. They laid him on a bier that had been filled with various kinds of spices prepared by the perfumer’s art; and they made a very great fire in his honor.
text.Rotherham: and they buried him in his own stately sepulchre, which he had hewn for himself in the city of David, and laid him on a couch which was full of sweet spices—yea of various kinds, made by the perfumer’s art,—and they burned for him with an exceeding great burning.
text.KJV: And they buried him in his own sepulchres, which he had made for himself in the city of David, and laid him in the bed which was filled with sweet odours and divers kinds of spices prepared by the apothecaries’ art: and they made a very great burning for him.
text.NABRE: They buried him in the tomb he had hewn for himself in the City of David, after laying him on a couch that was filled with spices and various kinds of aromatics compounded into an ointment; and they kindled a huge fire for him.
text.DRB: And they buried him in his sepulchre, which he had digged for himself in the City of David: and they laid him upon his bed full of spices and odoriferous ointments, which were made by the art of apothecaries, and they burnt over him with exceeding ambition.

text.NRSV: His son Jehoshaphat succeeded him, and strengthened himself against Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoshaphat his son reigned in his stead,—and strengthened himself against Israel;
text.KJV: And Jehoshaphat his son reigned in his stead, and strengthened himself against Israel.
text.NABRE: His son Jehoshaphat succeeded him as king and strengthened his position against Israel.
text.DRB: And Josaphat his son reigned for him, and grew strong against Israel.

text.NRSV: He placed forces in all the fortified cities of Judah, and set garrisons in the land of Judah, and in the cities of Ephraim that his father Asa had taken.
text.Rotherham: and put forces in all the fortified cities of Judah,—and set garrisons in the land of Judah, and in the cities of Ephraim, which Asa his father had captured.
text.KJV: And he placed forces in all the fenced cities of Judah, and set garrisons in the land of Judah, and in the cities of Ephraim, which Asa his father had taken.
text.NABRE: He placed armed forces in all the fortified cities of Judah, and set garrisons in the land of Judah and in the cities of Ephraim which Asa his father had taken.
text.DRB: And he appointed numbers of soldiers in all the cities of Juda, that were compassed with walls. And he placed garrisons in the land of Juda, & in the cities of Ephraim, which Asa his father had taken.

text.NRSV: The Lord was with Jehoshaphat, because he walked in the earlier ways of his father; he did not seek the Baals,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that, Yahweh, was with Jehoshaphat,—because he walked in the first ways of David his father, and sought not unto the Baalim;
text.KJV: And the Lord was with Jehoshaphat, because he walked in the first ways of his father David, and sought not unto Baalim;
text.NABRE: The Lord was with Jehoshaphat, for he walked in the earlier ways of David his father, and did not seek the Baals.
text.DRB: And our Lord was with Josaphat, because he walked in the first ways of David his father: and he trusted not in Baalim,

text.NRSV: but sought the God of his father and walked in his commandments, and not according to the ways of Israel.
text.Rotherham: for, unto the God of his father, he sought, and, in his commandments, he walked,—and not according to the doing of Israel.
text.KJV: But sought to the Lord God of his father, and walked in his commandments, and not after the doings of Israel.
text.NABRE: Rather, he sought the God of his father and walked in his commands, and not the practices of Israel.
text.DRB: but in the God of his father, & went forward in his precepts, and not according to the sins of Israel.

text.NRSV: Therefore the Lord established the kingdom in his hand. All Judah brought tribute to Jehoshaphat, and he had great riches and honor.
text.Rotherham: Therefore did Yahweh establish the kingdom in his hand, and all Judah gave a present unto Jehoshaphat,—and he came to have riches and honour, in abundance.
text.KJV: Therefore the Lord stablished the kingdom in his hand; and all Judah brought to Jehoshaphat presents; and he had riches and honour in abundance.
text.NABRE: Through him, the Lord made the kingdom secure, and all Judah gave Jehoshaphat gifts, so that great wealth and glory was his.
text.DRB: And our Lord confirmed the Kingdom in his hand, and all Juda gave gifts to Josaphat: and there grew to him infinite riches, and much glory.

text.NRSV: His heart was courageous in the ways of the Lord; and furthermore he removed the high places and the sacred poles from Judah.
text.Rotherham: And his heart was encouraged, in the ways of Yahweh,—and he, yet further, took away the high places and the Sacred Stems, out of Judah.
text.KJV: And his heart was lifted up in the ways of the Lord: moreover he took away the high places and groves out of Judah.
text.NABRE: Thus he was encouraged to follow the Lord’s ways, and once again he removed the high places and the asherahs from Judah.
text.DRB: And when his heart had taken courage for the ways of our Lord, he took away also the Excelses and groves out of Juda.

text.NRSV: In the third year of his reign he sent his officials, Ben-hail, Obadiah, Zechariah, Nethanel, and Micaiah, to teach in the cities of Judah.
text.Rotherham: And, in the third year of his reign, he sent to his rulers, even to Ben-hail and to Obadiah and to Zechariah, and to Nethanel, and to Micaiah,—that they were to teach throughout the cities of Judah;
text.KJV: Also in the third year of his reign he sent to his princes, even to Ben–hail, and to Obadiah, and to Zechariah, and to Nethaneel, and to Michaiah, to teach in the cities of Judah.
text.NABRE: In the third year of his reign he sent his officials, Ben-hail, Obadiah, Zechariah, Nethanel, and Micaiah, to teach in the cities of Judah.
text.DRB: And in the third year of his Kingdom, he sent of his Princes Benhail, and Abdias, and Zacharias, and Nathanael, and Micheas, that they should teach in the cities of Juda:

text.NRSV: With them were the Levites, Shemaiah, Nethaniah, Zebadiah, Asahel, Shemiramoth, Jehonathan, Adonijah, Tobijah, and Tob-adonijah; and with these Levites, the priests Elishama and Jehoram.
text.Rotherham: and, with them, Levites, Shemaiah and Nethaniah and Zebadiah and Asahel and Shemiramoth and Jehonathan, and Adonijah and Tobijah and Tob-adonijah, Levites,—and, with them, Elishama and Jehoram, priests;
text.KJV: And with them he sent Levites, even Shemaiah, and Nethaniah, and Zebadiah, and Asahel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehonathan, and Adonijah, and Tobijah, and Tob–adonijah, Levites; and with them Elishama and Jehoram, priests.
text.NABRE: With them he sent the Levites Shemaiah, Nethaniah, Zebadiah, Asahel, Shemiramoth, Jehonathan, Adonijah, and Tobijah, together with Elishama and Jehoram the priests.
text.DRB: and with them Levites, Semeias, & Nathanias, and Zabadias, Asael also, and Semiramoth, and Jonathan, and Adonias, and Tobias, and Thobadonias Levites, and with them Elisama, and Joram Priests.

text.NRSV: They taught in Judah, having the book of the law of the Lord with them; they went around through all the cities of Judah and taught among the people.
text.Rotherham: and they taught throughout Judah, and, with them, was the book of the law of Yahweh,—so they went round throughout all the cities of Judah, and taught the people.
text.KJV: And they taught in Judah, and had the book of the law of the Lord with them, and went about throughout all the cities of Judah, and taught the people.
text.NABRE: They taught in Judah, having with them the book of the law of the Lord; they traveled through all the cities of Judah and taught among the people.
text.DRB: And they taught the people in Juda, having the book of the law of our Lord: and they went about all the cities of Juda, and instructed the people.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord fell on all the kingdoms of the lands around Judah, and they did not make war against Jehoshaphat.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that, the dread of Yahweh, was upon all the kingdoms of the countries, that were round about Judah,—and they warred not against Jehoshaphat.
text.KJV: And the fear of the Lord fell upon all the kingdoms of the lands that were round about Judah, so that they made no war against Jehoshaphat.
text.NABRE: His Power. Now the fear of the Lord was upon all the kingdoms of the countries surrounding Judah, so that they did not war against Jehoshaphat.
text.DRB: Therefore the dread of our Lord came upon all the Kingdoms of the lands, that were round about Juda, neither durst they make battle against Josaphat.

text.NRSV: Some of the Philistines brought Jehoshaphat presents, and silver for tribute; and the Arabs also brought him seven thousand seven hundred rams and seven thousand seven hundred male goats.
text.Rotherham: And, from among the Philistines, were they bringing in unto Jehoshaphat a present, and silver as tribute,—even the Arabians, were bringing in unto him flocks, seven thousand seven hundred rams, and seven thousand seven hundred he-goats.
text.KJV: Also some of the Philistines brought Jehoshaphat presents, and tribute silver; and the Arabians brought him flocks, seven thousand and seven hundred rams, and seven thousand and seven hundred he goats.
text.NABRE: Some of the Philistines brought Jehoshaphat gifts and a tribute of silver; the Arabians also brought him a flock of seven thousand seven hundred rams and seven thousand seven hundred he-goats.
text.DRB: Yea and the Philistians brought gifts to Josaphat, and tribute of silver, the Arabians also brought cattle, of rams seven thousand seven hundred, and buck goats as many.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat grew steadily greater. He built fortresses and storage cities in Judah.
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass that, Jehoshaphat, went on waxing surpassingly great,—and he built, throughout Judah, fortresses and cities for store;
text.KJV: And Jehoshaphat waxed great exceedingly; and he built in Judah castles, and cities of store.
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat grew ever greater. He built strongholds and store cities in Judah.
text.DRB: Josaphat therefore grew, & was magnified on high: and he built in Juda houses like to towers, and walled cities.

text.NRSV: He carried out great works in the cities of Judah. He had soldiers, mighty warriors, in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: and, much business, had he, throughout the cities of Judah,—but, the men of war, the heroes of valour, were in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And he had much business in the cities of Judah: and the men of war, mighty men of valour, were in Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: He carried out many works in the cities of Judah, and he had soldiers, valiant warriors, in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And he prepared many works in the cities of Juda: there were also men of war, and valiant in Jerusalem,

text.NRSV: This was the muster of them by ancestral houses: Of Judah, the commanders of the thousands: Adnah the commander, with three hundred thousand mighty warriors,
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the numbers of them, by their ancestral house,—To Judah, pertained rulers of thousands, Adnah the chief, and, with him, mighty men of valour, three hundred thousand;
text.KJV: And these are the numbers of them according to the house of their fathers: Of Judah, the captains of thousands; Adnah the chief, and with him mighty men of valour three hundred thousand.
text.NABRE: This was their mustering according to their ancestral houses. From Judah, the commanders of thousands: Adnah the commander, and with him three hundred thousand valiant warriors.
text.DRB: of whom this is the number by the houses and families of every one: In Juda Princes of the army, Ednas duke, and with him most valiant men three hundred thousand.

text.NRSV: and next to him Jehohanan the commander, with two hundred eighty thousand,
text.Rotherham: and, under his direction, was Jehohanan the chief,—and, with him, two hundred and eighty thousand;
text.KJV: And next to him was Jehohanan the captain, and with him two hundred and fourscore thousand.
text.NABRE: Next to him, Jehohanan the commander, and with him two hundred eighty thousand.
text.DRB: After him Johanan the Prince, and with him two hundred eighty thousand.

text.NRSV: and next to him Amasiah son of Zichri, a volunteer for the service of the Lord, with two hundred thousand mighty warriors.
text.Rotherham: and, under his direction, Amasiah son of Zichri, who willingly offered himself unto Yahweh,—and, with him, two hundred thousand heroes of valour.
text.KJV: And next him was Amasiah the son of Zichri, who willingly offered himself unto the Lord; and with him two hundred thousand mighty men of valour.
text.NABRE: Next to him, Amasiah, son of Zichri, who offered himself to the Lord, and with him two hundred thousand valiant warriors.
text.DRB: After him also Amasias the son of Zechri, consecrated to our Lord, and with him two hundred thousand of valiant men.

text.NRSV: Of Benjamin: Eliada, a mighty warrior, with two hundred thousand armed with bow and shield,
text.Rotherham: And, out of Benjamin, a hero of valour, Eliada,—and, with him, armed with bow and buckler, two hundred thousand;
text.KJV: And of Benjamin; Eliada a mighty man of valour, and with him armed men with bow and shield two hundred thousand.
text.NABRE: From Benjamin: Eliada, a valiant warrior, and with him two hundred thousand armed with bow and buckler.
text.DRB: Him followed Eliada valiant to battles, and with him of them that held bow and shield two hundred thousand.

text.NRSV: and next to him Jehozabad with one hundred eighty thousand armed for war.
text.Rotherham: and, under his direction, Jehozabad,—and, with him, a hundred and eighty thousand, equipped for war.
text.KJV: And next him was Jehozabad, and with him an hundred and fourscore thousand ready prepared for the war.
text.NABRE: Next to him, Jehozabad, and with him one hundred and eighty thousand equipped for war.
text.DRB: After this man also Jozabad, and with him an hundred eighty thousand ready soldiers.

text.NRSV: These were in the service of the king, besides those whom the king had placed in the fortified cities throughout all Judah.
text.Rotherham: These, were they who were waiting upon the king,—besides those whom the king placed in the fortified cities, throughout all Judah.
text.KJV: These waited on the king, beside those whom the king put in the fenced cities throughout all Judah.
text.NABRE: These attended the king; in addition to those whom the king had stationed in the fortified cities throughout all Judah.
text.DRB: All these were at the hand of the king, beside others, whom he had put in walled cities, in all Juda.

text.NRSV: Now Jehoshaphat had great riches and honor; and he made a marriage alliance with Ahab.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jehoshaphat had riches and honour, in abundance, that he contracted, by marriage, affinity with Ahab.
text.KJV: Now Jehoshaphat had riches and honour in abundance, and joined affinity with Ahab.
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat therefore had wealth and glory in abundance; but he became related to Ahab by marriage.
text.DRB: Josaphat therefore was rich and very glorious, & was joined in affinity to Achab.

text.NRSV: After some years he went down to Ahab in Samaria. Ahab slaughtered an abundance of sheep and oxen for him and for the people who were with him, and induced him to go up against Ramoth-gilead.
text.Rotherham: So he went down, at the end of some years, unto Ahab, to Samaria, Ahab therefore sacrificed for him, sheep and oxen, in abundance, also for the people whom he had with him,-and then persuaded him to go up against Ramoth-gilead.
text.KJV: And after certain years he went down to Ahab to Samaria. And Ahab killed sheep and oxen for him in abundance, and for the people that he had with him, and persuaded him to go up with him to Ramoth–gilead.
text.NABRE: After some years he went down to Ahab at Samaria; Ahab slaughtered numerous sheep and oxen for him and for the people with him, and incited him to go up against Ramoth-gilead.
text.DRB: And he went down to him after certain years into Samaria: at whose coming Achab killed muttons and oxen very many for him and the people that came with him: & he persuaded him to go up into Ramoth Galaad.

text.NRSV: King Ahab of Israel said to King Jehoshaphat of Judah, “Will you go with me to Ramoth-gilead?” He answered him, “I am with you, my people are your people. We will be with you in the war.”
text.Rotherham: For Ahab king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat king of Judah, Wilt thou go up with me to Ramoth-gilead? And he said unto him—So am I, even as thou, and, like thy people, are my people, and, with thee, in the war.
text.KJV: And Ahab king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat king of Judah, Wilt thou go with me to Ramoth–gilead? And he answered him, I am as thou art, and my people as thy people; and we will be with thee in the war.
text.NABRE: Ahab, king of Israel, asked Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, “Will you come with me to Ramoth-gilead?” He answered, “You and I are as one, and your people and my people as well. We will be with you in the battle.”
text.DRB: And Achab the king of Israel said to Josaphat the king of Juda: Come with me into Ramoth Galaad. To whom he answered: As I am thou also: as thy people, so my people also: and we will be with thee in battle.

text.NRSV: But Jehoshaphat also said to the king of Israel, “Inquire first for the word of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Jehoshaphat, unto the king of Israel,—Seek, I pray thee, some time to-day, the word of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Jehoshaphat said unto the king of Israel, Enquire, I pray thee, at the word of the Lord to day.
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat also said to the king of Israel, “Seek the word of the Lord at once.”
text.DRB: Josaphat said to the king of Israel: Consult I beseech thee presently the word of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then the king of Israel gathered the prophets together, four hundred of them, and said to them, “Shall we go to battle against Ramoth-gilead, or shall I refrain?” They said, “Go up; for God will give it into the hand of the king.”
text.Rotherham: So the king of Israel gathered together the prophets, four hundred men, and said unto them, Shall we go up against Ramoth-gilead to battle, or shall I forbear? And they said—Go up, and may God deliver it into the hand of the king!
text.KJV: Therefore the king of Israel gathered together of prophets four hundred men, and said unto them, Shall we go to Ramoth–gilead to battle, or shall I forbear? And they said, Go up; for God will deliver it into the king’s hand.
text.NABRE: Prophets in Conflict. The king of Israel assembled the prophets, four hundred of them, and asked, “Shall we go to fight against Ramoth-gilead, or shall I refrain?” They said, “Attack. God will give it into the power of the king.”
text.DRB: Therefore the king of Israel gathered together of the Prophets four hundred men, & said to them: Shall we go into Ramoth Galaad to fight, or sit still? But they said: Go up, say they, & God will deliver it into thy hand.

text.NRSV: But Jehoshaphat said, “Is there no other prophet of the Lord here of whom we may inquire?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Jehoshaphat, Is there not here a prophet of Yahweh, besides,—that we may seek, from him?
text.KJV: But Jehoshaphat said, Is there not here a prophet of the Lord besides, that we might enquire of him?
text.NABRE: But Jehoshaphat said, “Is there no other prophet of the Lord here we might consult?”
text.DRB: And Josaphat said: Is there not here a Prophet of our Lord, that we may inquire also of him?

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “There is still one other by whom we may inquire of the Lord, Micaiah son of Imlah; but I hate him, for he never prophesies anything favorable about me, but only disaster.” Jehoshaphat said, “Let the king not say such a thing.”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king of Israel unto Jehoshaphat—There is yet one man, by whom to seek Yahweh, but, I, hate him, for he is never prophesying, concerning me, anything good—but—all his days—evil, the same, is Micaiah, son of Imla. Then said Jehoshaphat, Let not the king say, so!
text.KJV: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat, There is yet one man, by whom we may enquire of the Lord: but I hate him; for he never prophesied good unto me, but always evil: the same is Micaiah the son of Imla. And Jehoshaphat said, Let not the king say so.
text.NABRE: The king of Israel answered, “There is one other man through whom we may consult the Lord; but I hate him, because he prophesies not good but always evil about me. He is Micaiah, son of Imlah.” Jeshoshaphat said, “Let not the king say that.”
text.DRB: And the king of Israel said to Josaphat: There is one man, of whom we may ask the will of our Lord: but I hate him, because he doth not prophesy me good, but evil at all times: and it is Micheas the son of Jemla. And Josaphat said: Speak not in this manner, o king.

text.NRSV: Then the king of Israel summoned an officer and said, “Bring quickly Micaiah son of Imlah.”
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel called for a certain officer,—and said, Haste, Micaiah son of Imla!
text.KJV: And the king of Israel called for one of his officers, and said, Fetch quickly Micaiah the son of Imla.
text.NABRE: So the king of Israel called an official, and said to him, “Get Micaiah, son of Imlah, at once.”
text.DRB: The king of Israel therefore called one of the Eunuchs, and said to him: Call quickly Micheas the son of Jemla.

text.NRSV: Now the king of Israel and King Jehoshaphat of Judah were sitting on their thrones, arrayed in their robes; and they were sitting at the threshing floor at the entrance of the gate of Samaria; and all the prophets were prophesying before them.
text.Rotherham: Now, the king of Israel, and Jehoshaphat king of Judah, were sitting, each upon his throne, clothed in robes, and they were sitting in an open space, at the entrance of the gate of Samaria,—and, all the prophets, were prophesying before them.
text.KJV: And the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat king of Judah sat either of them on his throne, clothed in their robes, and they sat in a void place at the entering in of the gate of Samaria; and all the prophets prophesied before them.
text.NABRE: The king of Israel and Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, were seated, each on his throne, clothed in their robes of state in the square at the entrance of the gate of Samaria, and all the prophets were prophesying before them.
text.DRB: Moreover the king of Israel, and Josaphat the king of Juda, both sat in their thrones, clothed with kingly attire, and they sat in the court beside the gate of Samaria, and all the Prophets prophesied before them.

text.NRSV: Zedekiah son of Chenaanah made for himself horns of iron, and he said, “Thus says the Lord: With these you shall gore the Arameans until they are destroyed.”
text.Rotherham: Then did Zedekiah son of Chenaanah make him horns of iron,—and said—Thus, saith Yahweh, With these, shalt thou push Syria, until they are consumed.
text.KJV: And Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah had made him horns of iron, and said, Thus saith the Lord, With these thou shalt push Syria until they be consumed.
text.NABRE: Zedekiah, son of Chenaanah, made himself two horns of iron and said: “The Lord says: With these you shall gore Aram until you have destroyed them.”
text.DRB: But Sedecias the son of Chanaana made him horns of iron, and said: Thus saith our Lord: With these shalt thou strike Syria, till thou destroy it.

text.NRSV: All the prophets were prophesying the same and saying, “Go up to Ramoth-gilead and triumph; the Lord will give it into the hand of the king.”
text.Rotherham: And, all the prophets, were prophesying, so, saying,-Go up to Ramoth-gilead, and prosper, and Yahweh will deliver [it] into the hand of the king.
text.KJV: And all the prophets prophesied so, saying, Go up to Ramoth–gilead, and prosper: for the Lord shall deliver it into the hand of the king.
text.NABRE: The other prophets prophesied in the same vein, saying: “Attack Ramoth-gilead, and conquer! The Lord will give it into the power of the king.”
text.DRB: And all the Prophets in like manner prophesied, and said: Go up into Ramoth Galaad, and thou shalt prosper, and our Lord will deliver them into the king's hand.

text.NRSV: The messenger who had gone to summon Micaiah said to him, “Look, the words of the prophets with one accord are favorable to the king; let your word be like the word of one of them, and speak favorably.”
text.Rotherham: And, the messenger who went to call Micaiah, spake unto him, saying, Lo! the words of the prophets, with one mouth, are good for the king,—be thy word then, I pray thee, like one of theirs, so wilt thou speak good.
text.KJV: And the messenger that went to call Micaiah spake to him, saying, Behold, the words of the prophets declare good to the king with one assent; let thy word therefore, I pray thee, be like one of theirs, and speak thou good.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile the messenger who had gone to call Micaiah said to him: “Look now, the words of the prophets are as one in speaking good for the king. Let your word be at one with theirs; speak a good word.”
text.DRB: And the messenger that went to call Micheas, said to him: Behold the words of all the Prophets with one mouth tell the king good things: I beseech thee therefore that thy word also dissent not from them, and that thou speak prosperous things.

text.NRSV: But Micaiah said, “As the Lord lives, whatever my God says, that I will speak.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Micaiah,—By the life of Yahweh, whatsoever my God shall say, that, must I speak.
text.KJV: And Micaiah said, As the Lord liveth, even what my God saith, that will I speak.
text.NABRE: Micaiah said, “As the Lord lives, I shall speak whatever my God says.”
text.DRB: To whom Micheas answered: Our Lord liveth, whatsoever my God shall say to me, that will I speak.

text.NRSV: When he had come to the king, the king said to him, “Micaiah, shall we go to Ramoth-gilead to battle, or shall I refrain?” He answered, “Go up and triumph; they will be given into your hand.”
text.Rotherham: Now, when he came unto the king, the king said unto him, Micah, shall we go against Ramoth-gilead to battle, or shall I forbear? And he said, Go ye up and prosper, and may they be delivered into your hand.
text.KJV: And when he was come to the king, the king said unto him, Micaiah, shall we go to Ramoth–gilead to battle, or shall I forbear? And he said, Go ye up, and prosper, and they shall be delivered into your hand.
text.NABRE: When he came to the king, the king said to him, “Micah, shall we go to fight at Ramoth-gilead, or shall I refrain?” He said, “Attack and conquer! They will be delivered into your power.”
text.DRB: He therefore came to the king. To whom the king said: Micheas, shall we go into Ramoth Galaad to fight, or sit still? To whom he answered Go ye up: for all things shall fall out prosperous, and the enemies shall be delivered into your hands.

text.NRSV: But the king said to him, “How many times must I make you swear to tell me nothing but the truth in the name of the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: Then the king said unto him, How many times, must, I, be putting thee on oath,—that thou speak unto me nothing but the truth in the name of Yahweh?
text.KJV: And the king said to him, How many times shall I adjure thee that thou say nothing but the truth to me in the name of the Lord?
text.NABRE: But the king answered him, “How many times must I adjure you to tell me nothing but the truth in the name of the Lord?”
text.DRB: And the king said: Again and again I adjure thee, that thou speak not to me, but that which is true in the name of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Micaiah said, “I saw all Israel scattered on the mountains, like sheep without a shepherd; and the Lord said, ‘These have no master; let each one go home in peace.’”
text.Rotherham: So he said, I saw all Israel, scattered upon the mountains, like sheep that have no shepherd,-and Yahweh said, No masters, have these! let them return every man unto his own house, in peace.
text.KJV: Then he said, I did see all Israel scattered upon the mountains, as sheep that have no shepherd: and the Lord said, These have no master; let them return therefore every man to his house in peace.
text.NABRE: So Micaiah said: “I see all Israel scattered on the mountains, like sheep without a shepherd, And the Lord saying, These have no masters! Let each of them go back home in peace.”
text.DRB: But he said: I saw all Israel disperst in the mountains, as sheep without a shepherd: & our Lord said: These have no masters: let every man return into his house in peace.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “Did I not tell you that he would not prophesy anything favorable about me, but only disaster?”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king of Israel, unto Jehoshaphat,—Said I not unto thee, He wilt not prophesy concerning me anything good, but evil?
text.KJV: And the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, Did I not tell thee that he would not prophesy good unto me, but evil?
text.NABRE: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “Did I not tell you, he does not prophesy good about me, but only evil?”
text.DRB: And the king of Israel said to Josaphat: did I not tell thee that this man did not prophesy me any good, but those things that be evil?

text.NRSV: Then Micaiah said, “Therefore hear the word of the Lord: I saw the Lord sitting on his throne, with all the host of heaven standing to the right and to the left of him.
text.Rotherham: Then said he, Therefore, hear ye the word of Yahweh,—I saw Yahweh, sitting upon his throne, and, all the host of the heavens, standing, on his right hand and his left.
text.KJV: Again he said, Therefore hear the word of the Lord; I saw the Lord sitting upon his throne, and all the host of heaven standing on his right hand and on his left.
text.NABRE: Micaiah continued: “Therefore hear the word of the Lord. I saw the Lord seated on his throne, with the whole host of heaven standing to his right and to his left.
text.DRB: But he said: Hear ye therefore the word of our Lord: I saw our Lord sitting in his throne, and all the host of heaven assisting him on the right hand and on the left.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said, ‘Who will entice King Ahab of Israel, so that he may go up and fall at Ramoth-gilead?’ Then one said one thing, and another said another,
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh, Who will persuade Ahab king of Israel, that he may go up and fall in Ramoth-gilead? And one spake, saying after this manner, and another, saying after that manner.
text.KJV: And the Lord said, Who shall entice Ahab king of Israel, that he may go up and fall at Ramoth–gilead? And one spake saying after this manner, and another saying after that manner.
text.NABRE: The Lord asked: Who will deceive Ahab, king of Israel, so that he will go up and fall on Ramoth-gilead? And one said this, another that,
text.DRB: And our Lord said: who shall deceive Achab the king of Israel, that he may go up and fall in Ramoth Galaad? And when one said in this manner, and another otherwise:

text.NRSV: until a spirit came forward and stood before the Lord, saying, ‘I will entice him.’ The Lord asked him, ‘How?’
text.Rotherham: Then came there forth a spirit and stood before Yahweh, and said, I, will persuade him. And Yahweh said unto him, Wherewith?
text.KJV: Then there came out a spirit, and stood before the Lord, and said, I will entice him. And the Lord said unto him, Wherewith?
text.NABRE: until this spirit came forth and stood before the Lord, saying, ‘I will deceive him.’ The Lord asked: How?
text.DRB: there came forth a spirit, and stood before our Lord, & said: I will deceive him. To whom our Lord said: wherein wilt thou deceive him.

text.NRSV: He replied, ‘I will go out and be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets.’ Then the Lord said, ‘You are to entice him, and you shall succeed; go out and do it.’
text.Rotherham: And he said, I will go forth and become a spirit of falsehood, in the mouth of all his prophets. And he said—Thou mayest persuade, moreover also, thou shalt prevail, go forth, and do, so.
text.KJV: And he said, I will go out, and be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets. And the Lord said, Thou shalt entice him, and thou shalt also prevail: go out, and do even so.
text.NABRE: He answered, ‘I will go forth and become a lying spirit in the mouths of all his prophets.’ The Lord replied: You shall succeed in deceiving him. Go forth and do this.
text.DRB: But he answered: I will go forth, and will be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his Prophets. And our Lord said: Thou shalt deceive, and shalt prevail: go forth, and do so.

text.NRSV: So you see, the Lord has put a lying spirit in the mouth of these your prophets; the Lord has decreed disaster for you.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! Yahweh hath suffered a spirit of falsehood to be put into the mouth of these thy prophets,—but, Yahweh, hath spoken concerning thee—evil.
text.KJV: Now therefore, behold, the Lord hath put a lying spirit in the mouth of these thy prophets, and the Lord hath spoken evil against thee.
text.NABRE: So now the Lord has put a lying spirit in the mouths of these prophets of yours; but the Lord himself has decreed evil against you.”
text.DRB: Now therefore, behold our Lord hath given the spirit of lying in the mouth of all thy Prophets, and our Lord hath spoken of thee evil things.

text.NRSV: Then Zedekiah son of Chenaanah came up to Micaiah, slapped him on the cheek, and said, “Which way did the spirit of the Lord pass from me to speak to you?”
text.Rotherham: Then drew near Zedekiah son of Chenaanah, and smote Micaiah upon the cheek,—and said, Which then is the way the spirit of Yahweh passed from me, to speak with thee?
text.KJV: Then Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah came near, and smote Micaiah upon the cheek, and said, Which way went the Spirit of the Lord from me to speak unto thee?
text.NABRE: Thereupon Zedekiah, son of Chenaanah, came up and struck Micaiah on the cheek, saying, “Has the spirit of the Lord, then, passed from me to speak with you?”
text.DRB: And Sedecias the son of Chanaana came, and struck the cheek of Micheas, and said: Which way passed the spirit of our Lord from me, that it should speak to thee.

text.NRSV: Micaiah replied, “You will find out on that day when you go in to hide in an inner chamber.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Micaiah, Lo! thou shalt see, on that day when thou shalt enter into a chamber within a chamber, to hide thyself.
text.KJV: And Micaiah said, Behold, thou shalt see on that day when thou shalt go into an inner chamber to hide thyself.
text.NABRE: Micaiah said, “You shall find out on the day you go into an innermost room to hide.”
text.DRB: And Micheas said: Thou thyself shalt see in that day, when thou shalt enter into chamber out of chamber, to be hid.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel then ordered, “Take Micaiah, and return him to Amon the governor of the city and to Joash the king’s son;
text.Rotherham: Then said the king of Israel, Take ye Micaiah, and carry him back unto Amon ruler of the city, and unto Joash son of the king;
text.KJV: Then the king of Israel said, Take ye Micaiah, and carry him back to Amon the governor of the city, and to Joash the king’s son;
text.NABRE: The king of Israel then said: “Seize Micaiah and take him back to Amon, prefect of the city, and to Joash the king’s son,
text.DRB: And the king of Israel commanded, saying: Take Micheas, and lead him to Amon the governor of the city, and to Joas the son of Amelech.

text.NRSV: and say, ‘Thus says the king: Put this fellow in prison, and feed him on reduced rations of bread and water until I return in peace.’”
text.Rotherham: and ye shall say, Thus, saith the king, Put ye this one into the prison,—and feed him with the bread of oppression, and with the water of oppression, until I return in peace.
text.KJV: And say, Thus saith the king, Put this fellow in the prison, and feed him with bread of affliction and with water of affliction, until I return in peace.
text.NABRE: and say, ‘This is the king’s order: Put this man in prison and feed him scanty rations of bread and water until I come back in safety!’”
text.DRB: And you shall say: Thus saith the king: Cast this fellow into prison, and give him a little bread, and a little water, till I return in peace.

text.NRSV: Micaiah said, “If you return in peace, the Lord has not spoken by me.” And he said, “Hear, you peoples, all of you!”
text.Rotherham: Then said Micaiah, If thou do, at all return, in peace, Yahweh hath not spoken by me. And he said, Hear! ye peoples, all!
text.KJV: And Micaiah said, If thou certainly return in peace, then hath not the Lord spoken by me. And he said, Hearken, all ye people.
text.NABRE: But Micaiah said, “If ever you return in safety, the Lord has not spoken through me.” (He also said, “Hear, O peoples, all of you!”)
text.DRB: And Micheas said: If thou return in peace, our Lord hath not spoken in me, and he said: Hear all ye peoples.

text.NRSV: So the king of Israel and King Jehoshaphat of Judah went up to Ramoth-gilead.
text.Rotherham: Then went up the king of Israel, and Jehoshaphat king of Judah, against Ramothgilead.
text.KJV: So the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat the king of Judah went up to Ramoth–gilead.
text.NABRE: Ahab’s Death. The king of Israel and Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, went up to Ramoth-gilead,
text.DRB: Therefore the king of Israel, & Josaphat the king of Juda, went up into Ramoth Galaad.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “I will disguise myself and go into battle, but you wear your robes.” So the king of Israel disguised himself, and they went into battle.
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat, [I am about] to disguise myself, and enter into the battle, but, thou, put on thy robes. So the king of Israel disguised himself, and entered into the battle.
text.KJV: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat, I will disguise myself, and will go to the battle; but put thou on thy robes. So the king of Israel disguised himself; and they went to the battle.
text.NABRE: and the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “I will disguise myself and go into battle. But you, put on your own robes.” So the king of Israel disguised himself and they entered the battle.
text.DRB: And the king of Israel said to Josaphat: I will change my habit, and so I will go to the fight, but be thou clothed with thine own garments. And the king of Israel changing his habit came to the battle.

text.NRSV: Now the king of Aram had commanded the captains of his chariots, “Fight with no one small or great, but only with the king of Israel.”
text.Rotherham: Now, the king of Syria, had commanded the captains of chariots which he had, saying, Ye shall not fight, with small [or] with great,—save with the king of Israel, alone.
text.KJV: Now the king of Syria had commanded the captains of the chariots that were with him, saying, Fight ye not with small or great, save only with the king of Israel.
text.NABRE: In the meantime, the king of Aram had given his chariot commanders the order, “Fight with no one, great or small, except the king of Israel alone.”
text.DRB: And the king of Syria had commanded the captains of his horsemen saying: Fight not against the least, or against the greatest, but against the king of Israel only.

text.NRSV: When the captains of the chariots saw Jehoshaphat, they said, “It is the king of Israel.” So they turned to fight against him; and Jehoshaphat cried out, and the Lord helped him. God drew them away from him,
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass, when the chariot-captains saw Jehoshaphat, that, they, said, The king of Israel, it is. And they compassed him about, to fight,—but Jehoshaphat made outcry, and, Yahweh, helped him, yea God allured them away from him.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when the captains of the chariots saw Jehoshaphat, that they said, It is the king of Israel. Therefore they compassed about him to fight: but Jehoshaphat cried out, and the Lord helped him; and God moved them to depart from him.
text.NABRE: When the chariot commanders saw Jehoshaphat, they thought, “There is the king of Israel!” and wheeled to fight him. But Jehoshaphat cried out and the Lord helped him; God induced them to leave him alone.
text.DRB: Therefore when the Princes of the horsemen saw Josaphat, they said: This is the king of Israel. And they compassed him fighting: but he cried to our Lord, and he holp him, and turned them away from him.

text.NRSV: for when the captains of the chariots saw that it was not the king of Israel, they turned back from pursuing him.
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass, when the chariot-captains saw that it was not the king of Israel, that they turned back from pursuing him;
text.KJV: For it came to pass, that, when the captains of the chariots perceived that it was not the king of Israel, they turned back again from pursuing him.
text.NABRE: The chariot commanders, seeing that he was not the king of Israel, turned away from him.
text.DRB: For when the captains of the horsemen saw, that it was not the king of Israel, they left him.

text.NRSV: But a certain man drew his bow and unknowingly struck the king of Israel between the scale armor and the breastplate; so he said to the driver of his chariot, “Turn around, and carry me out of the battle, for I am wounded.”
text.Rotherham: but, a certain man, drawing a bow in his innocence, smote the king of Israel between the shoulder-joints and the coat of mail,—wherefore he said to the charioteer, Turn thy hand, and convey me out of the host, for I am sore wounded.
text.KJV: And a certain man drew a bow at a venture, and smote the king of Israel between the joints of the harness: therefore he said to his chariot man, Turn thine hand, that thou mayest carry me out of the host; for I am wounded.
text.NABRE: But someone drew his bow at random and hit the king of Israel between the joints of his breastplate. He ordered his charioteer, “Rein about and take me out of the ranks, for I am wounded.”
text.DRB: And it chanced that one of the people shot an arrow at adventure, and struck the king of Israel between the neck & the shoulders, & he said to his coacher: Turn thy hand, & carry me out of the battle, because I am wounded.

text.NRSV: The battle grew hot that day, and the king of Israel propped himself up in his chariot facing the Arameans until evening; then at sunset he died.
